rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical (3+2) cyclization between primary amides and olefins to give 2-oxazolines, using 1,2-dichloroethane (DCE) as both solvent and chloride source via paired electrolysis
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of DCE generates chloride anion (with ethylene + Cl- released); chloride is then oxidized at the anode to a Cl+ equivalent (chlorine radical/cation) that activates the alkene as a chloronium intermediate; protons are reduced at the cathode to H2

key_intermediates.name: chloride anion (from cathodic reduction of DCE)
chlorine radical / Cl+ equivalent (anodic oxidation of Cl-)
chloronium ion (3-membered chloriranium from alkene + Cl+)

electron_flow: At the cathode, DCE is reduced (2 e-) to release ethylene and 2 Cl-; at the anode, Cl- is oxidized to Cl+/Cl• which forms a chloronium with the alkene. The amide nitrogen opens the chloronium to give a beta-chloro amide, and intramolecular O-attack with loss of HCl furnishes the oxazoline; H+ is reduced at the cathode to H2.
rate_determining_step: Electrochemical redox step (first-order dependence on current; zero-order in indene and benzamide)

probe_reagents.name: AgNO3 (chloride detection)
probe_reagents.observation: White AgCl precipitate formed from the electrolyzed solution, confirming Cl- generation; solution pH dropped to 3.19

probe_reagents.name: styrene (alkene without intramolecular bias)
probe_reagents.observation: Base-free electrolysis gave beta-chloro amides 5bc (40%) and 5bc' (41%); resubjection with CH3COONa converted them to oxazolines 4bc/4bc' (35%/33%), supporting them as on-cycle intermediates

probe_reagents.name: HCl (alternative chloride source)
probe_reagents.observation: In THF (no DCE), 10 mol% HCl raised yield of 3aa from 11% to 51% and improved site selectivity (r.r. 10:1), confirming the essential role of Cl-

probe_reagents.name: current variation
probe_reagents.observation: Initial rate is first-order in current; zero-order in indene and benzamide -> electrochemical step is rate-controlling

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: DCE reduction at -1.5 V vs Ag/AgCl; Cl- oxidation at 1.5 V, below 1a (1.8 V) and 2a, so Cl- is preferentially oxidized at the anode

notes: An alternative mechanism (Figure S9) cannot be excluded: direct anodic oxidation of the alkene to a radical cation A, trapped by the amide to give benzyl radical B, further oxidized to benzyl cation C, then cyclization/deprotonation to the oxazoline. An induction period was observed in the time-monitored conversion. Thiophene-, furan-, 4-vinylpyridine-, cyclohexene-, cyclohexenone-, p-nitro/p-cyanostyrene-, and urea-type substrates were unsuitable (N.D.).
